# Transcrição: Why_Don't_Totals_Appear_in_Some_Facebook_Ads_Manager_Columns_

## [00:00 - 02:56]

This is the pubcast shot. Why is this happening? I'm John Loomer. Let's go. Have you ever noticed that sometimes but not always metric totals don't appear in ads manager? So you'll just see dashes at the bottom even though you have metrics happening so totals coming within the individual rows. It's super frustrating for many advertisers since this seems like an intermittent issue that is inconsistent and doesn't make sense. But there's a reason for it so let's discuss why this happens and what you should do about it. So first up this is a rather new development during the past year or so. So this happens when you utilize multiple attribution settings across different campaigns different ad sets. So if one campaign reports on conversions within a seven-day click and one-day view window and another one reports based on a one-day click window Facebook will not attempt to add these metrics the conversion metrics. So you'll see totals for other metrics you know reach and spend and all that kind of stuff but not conversions. This includes the standard events custom events and custom conversions. So what can you do? Well first know that the attribution setting is set within the ad set of conversions campaigns. You probably know that if you're having this issue because you would have said it differently. You can find the attribution setting under optimization and delivery so expand show more options in order to see it. As for your ads manager reporting click the columns drop down and select customize columns then add a column for attribution setting. You'll then be able to see whether you're using different attribution settings for different ad sets or campaigns. If you are consider viewing only those campaigns or ad sets utilizing the same attribution settings so group them together. Now within the filters select attribution setting and then check the box next to the setting that the campaigns you want to view are using. Hope that helps. Hey I'm doing something a little different in 2022. It's a free monthly webinar. The five Facebook ads tips every single month. I'm going to cover five basic tips related to targeting optimization attribution and reporting strategy business and account. Go sign up JohnLimber.com slash webinar. You can get access to all the prior episodes or any of the future ones as well. Thanks for joining me. Until next time do awesome things. I'm out.
